articles.title: Chatbots are now prescribing psychiatric drugs
articles.summary: Utah allows an AI chatbot to prescribe psychiatric drugs without doctor oversight in a pilot program aimed at reducing costs and addressing care shortages. Physicians raise concerns about the system's opacity and effectiveness in expanding mental health care access.

articles.title: PSA: Anyone with a link can view your Granola notes by default
articles.summary: Granola's AI note-taking app makes notes viewable to anyone with a link by default and uses them for AI training unless users opt out, despite claiming privacy-first defaults.

articles.title: Elon Musk is about to be a very busy boy!
articles.summary: Elon Musk may file for SpaceX's IPO in early April, with the earliest public offering expected in June pending SEC review of the S-1 filing.

articles.title: OpenAI acquires TBPN, the buzzy founder-led business talk show
articles.summary: OpenAI acquired TBPN, a popular Silicon Valley tech podcast. The show will maintain independence while being overseen by OpenAI's chief political operative Chris Lehane.

articles.title: Microsoft takes on AI rivals with three new foundational models
articles.summary: Microsoft released three new foundational AI models capable of transcribing voice to text and generating audio and images, six months after forming the group.

articles.title: Google now lets you direct avatars through prompts in its Vids app
articles.summary: Google's Vids app now allows users to customize and direct avatars through text prompts for video creation, enhancing the app's video production capabilities.

articles.title: Railway secures $100 million to challenge AWS with AI-native cloud infrastructure
articles.summary: Railway, a San Francisco cloud platform with 2 million developers, secured $100 million Series B funding to build AI-native infrastructure challenging AWS and Google Cloud.

articles.title: Claude Code costs up to $200 a month. Goose does the same thing for free.
articles.summary: Claude Code charges $20-$200 monthly for AI-powered coding, but Goose offers identical functionality for free as an open-source local alternative with no subscription fees.

articles.title: Listen Labs raises $69M after viral billboard hiring stunt to scale AI customer interviews
articles.summary: Listen Labs raised $69M Series B after a viral billboard hiring campaign in San Francisco. The startup used AI-encoded tokens and a coding challenge to recruit engineers, attracting thousands of applicants.

articles.title: New Rowhammer attacks give complete control of machines running Nvidia GPUs
articles.summary: Novel Rowhammer attacks on Nvidia GPUs allow malicious users in shared cloud environments to gain full root control of host machines by exploiting memory bit flip vulnerabilities in high-performance GPU cards.

articles.title: Quantum computers need vastly fewer resources than thought to break vital encryption
articles.summary: New research shows quantum computers could break elliptic curve encryption with far fewer resources than previously thought, with one approach cracking 256-bit ECC in 10 days using 100 times less overhead.

articles.title: Google bumps up Q Day deadline to 2029, far sooner than previously thought
articles.summary: Google moved up its Q Day deadline to 2029, when quantum computers could break current cryptography. The company urges global adoption of post-quantum cryptography algorithms.

articles.title: The Download: plastic’s problem with fuel prices, and SpaceX’s blockbuster IPO
articles.summary: Rising fuel prices from global conflicts threaten plastic supply chains and costs. Petrochemical-dependent plastic production faces economic ripple effects that could impact consumers.

articles.title: Fuel prices are soaring. Plastic could be next.
articles.summary: Rising oil prices from Middle East tensions are driving up gasoline costs and threatening plastic supply chains, as petrochemical production becomes increasingly constrained and expensive.

articles.title: The Download: gig workers training humanoids, and better AI benchmarks
articles.summary: Gig workers worldwide are training humanoid robots by recording themselves performing daily tasks with smartphones. Companies like Micro1 collect and sell this video data to robotics firms developing humanoids.

articles.title: Welcome Gemma 4: Frontier multimodal intelligence on device
articles.summary: Google introduces Gemma 4, a frontier multimodal AI model designed to run efficiently on-device. The advancement enables sophisticated AI intelligence directly on personal devices without requiring cloud connectivity.

articles.title: Holo3: Breaking the Computer Use Frontier
articles.summary: Holo3 represents a breakthrough in computer use AI, advancing how artificial intelligence interacts with digital interfaces and automates complex tasks.

articles.title: Falcon Perception
articles.summary: Falcon Perception advances AI vision capabilities with improved object detection and spatial understanding. The technology enhances machine perception for autonomous systems and real-world applications.

articles.title: A conversation with Kevin Scott: What’s next in AI
articles.summary: Kevin Scott discusses the future direction of AI technology and upcoming developments in the field.

articles.title: From Hot Wheels to handling content: How brands are using Microsoft AI to be more productive and imaginative
articles.summary: Brands leverage Microsoft AI tools to enhance productivity and creativity, enabling more efficient content creation and imaginative marketing strategies across various industries.

articles.title: Microsoft open sources its ‘farm of the future’ toolkit
articles.summary: Microsoft open sources its 'farm of the future' toolkit, making agricultural technology more accessible to developers and farmers for sustainable farming practices.

articles.title: New ways to balance cost and reliability in the Gemini API
articles.summary: Google introduces two new Gemini API inference tiers—Flex and Priority—to help developers balance cost efficiency with latency performance.

articles.title: Create, edit and share videos at no cost in Google Vids
articles.summary: Google Vids introduces free AI video creation and editing powered by Lyria 3 and Veo 3.1, enabling high-quality video generation at no cost.

articles.title: We’re creating a new satellite imagery map to help protect Brazil’s forests.
articles.summary: Google partnered with the Brazilian government to create a satellite imagery map for forest protection. The technology enables better monitoring and conservation of Brazil's forests.

articles.title: Gemma 4: Byte for byte, the most capable open models
articles.summary: Google releases Gemma 4, its most capable open-source models designed for advanced reasoning and agentic workflows, offering improved intelligence and performance.

articles.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live: Making audio AI more natural and reliable
articles.summary: Google's Gemini 3.1 Flash Live voice model enhances audio AI with improved precision and lower latency for more natural, fluid voice interactions.

articles.title: Protecting people from harmful manipulation
articles.summary: Google DeepMind researches how AI systems could manipulate people in sensitive domains like finance and healthcare, implementing new safety measures to prevent harmful outcomes.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #238 - GPT 5.4 mini, OpenAI Pivot, Mamba 3, Attention Residuals
articles.summary: OpenAI released GPT-5.4 mini and nano models with 400k-token context windows and improved token efficiency. Mistral open-sourced the Small 4 model family with MoE architecture.

articles.title: Last Week in AI #339 - DLSS 5, OpenAI Superapp, MiniMax M2.7
articles.summary: Nvidia unveiled DLSS 5, a generative AI graphics technology that enhances photorealism in real-time gaming up to 4K by analyzing scene semantics and lighting to generate detailed visuals.

articles.title: LWiAI Podcast #237 - Nemotron 3 Super, xAI reborn, Anthropic Lawsuit, Research!
articles.summary: Episode 237 discusses major AI developments including Perplexity's local Mac-based AI agent, Anthropic's GitHub PR code review pricing, and new interactive visualization features from ChatGPT and Anthropic.

articles.title: KiloClaw targets shadow AI with autonomous agent governance
articles.summary: KiloClaw is a new enterprise platform designed to govern autonomous agents and manage shadow AI deployments. It addresses the 'Bring Your Own AI' trend where employees bypass official procurement, exposing proprietary data to unregulated environments.

articles.title: 5 best practices to secure AI systems
articles.summary: AI systems require multi-layered security strategies including data protection, access control, and monitoring. Key practices include enforcing strict access governance, encryption, and role-based permissions to protect sensitive models.

articles.title: China’s Five-Year Plan details the targets for AI deployment
articles.summary: China's 15th Five-Year Plan prioritizes AI development through 2030, targeting high-performance chips, software, and new model architectures alongside quantum computing and biotechnology. Advanced communications technologies like 5G+ and 6G will support AI deployment.

articles.title: Which Agent Causes Task Failures and When?Researchers from PSU and Duke explores automated failure attribution of LLM Multi-Agent Systems
articles.summary: Researchers from PSU and Duke developed methods to identify which agent causes task failures in LLM multi-agent systems and when failures occur, addressing a key challenge in collaborative AI systems.

articles.title: ByteDance Introduces Astra: A Dual-Model Architecture for Autonomous Robot Navigation
articles.summary: ByteDance introduced Astra, a dual-model architecture for autonomous robot navigation that addresses localization, destination identification, and path planning challenges in complex indoor environments.

articles.title: MIT Researchers Unveil “SEAL”: A New Step Towards Self-Improving AI
articles.summary: MIT researchers introduced SEAL, a framework enabling large language models to self-improve by generating training data through self-editing and updating their own weights using reinforcement learning.

articles.title: How GhostClaw malware targets the OpenClaw AI agent boom
articles.summary: GhostClaw malware exploits macOS binaries to steal credentials from developers running local AI agents on Mac Minis, targeting the growing OpenClaw AI agent ecosystem.

articles.title: Why Meta’s V-JEPA 2.1 model is a massive step forward for real-world AI
articles.summary: Meta's V-JEPA 2.1 advances video self-supervised learning by solving the long-standing challenge of balancing motion tracking with spatial detail in AI models.

articles.title: Multi-level AI prompt engineering: A new tool for scientific discovery
articles.summary: Multi-level prompt engineering and parabolic extrapolation enable LLMs to function as theoretical collaborators, producing testable multiverse models for scientific discovery.

articles.title: TII Releases Falcon Perception: A 0.6B-Parameter Early-Fusion Transformer for Open-Vocabulary Grounding and Segmentation from Natural Language Prompts
articles.summary: TII released Falcon Perception, a 600M-parameter unified Transformer that processes image patches and text tokens together from the first layer for efficient open-vocabulary grounding and segmentation tasks.

articles.title: Step by Step Guide to Build an End-to-End Model Optimization Pipeline with NVIDIA Model Optimizer Using FastNAS Pruning and Fine-Tuning
articles.summary: This tutorial demonstrates building an end-to-end model optimization pipeline using NVIDIA Model Optimizer with FastNAS pruning and fine-tuning on ResNet and CIFAR-10 dataset in Google Colab.

articles.title: Arcee AI Releases Trinity Large Thinking: An Apache 2.0 Open Reasoning Model for Long-Horizon Agents and Tool Use
articles.summary: Arcee AI released Trinity Large Thinking, an open-source reasoning model under Apache 2.0 license designed for complex multi-step reasoning, autonomous agents, and tool use.

articles.title: I tested the Bloom Card to cut my screen time: It beats Brick on features and price, but it's easier to bypass
articles.summary: Bloom Card helps reduce screen time with easy scheduling and affordable pricing, but its engaging interface and short breaks may tempt users to bypass restrictions.

articles.title: Do Apple's new AirPods Max 2 beat the Airpods Pro 3? I've tried both, here's my take
articles.summary: Apple's AirPods Max 2 are premium over-ear headphones, but may not suit everyone compared to AirPods Pro 3. The choice depends on individual preferences and use cases.

articles.title: I turned to PrivacyBee to clean up my data - here's how it made me disappear
articles.summary: PrivacyBee is a comprehensive data removal service that effectively deletes personal information across hundreds of websites, helping users achieve digital privacy.

articles.title: 7 Machine Learning Trends to Watch in 2026
articles.summary: Machine learning systems are evolving from backend dashboards into more visible applications. The article outlines seven key ML trends expected to shape 2026's technological landscape.

articles.title: Building a ‘Human-in-the-Loop’ Approval Gate for Autonomous Agents
articles.summary: The article discusses state-managed interruptions in agentic AI systems, where human approval gates intentionally halt agent execution pipelines to maintain oversight and control.

articles.title: From Prompt to Prediction: Understanding Prefill, Decode, and the KV Cache in LLMs
articles.summary: Article explains LLM inference stages: prefill phase where attention processes prompts, decode phase generating predictions token-by-token, and KV cache optimization for efficient decoding.

articles.title: I Replaced Vector DBs with Google’s Memory Agent Pattern for my notes in Obsidian
articles.summary: Developer replaced vector databases with Google's Memory Agent Pattern for Obsidian notes, eliminating need for embeddings and complex similarity search infrastructure.

articles.title: Linear Regression Is Actually a Projection Problem (Part 2: From Projections to Predictions)
articles.summary: Linear regression can be understood as a projection problem using vector mathematics. This geometric perspective explains least squares from projections to predictions.

articles.title: How to Handle Classical Data in Quantum Models
articles.summary: The article explores workflows and encoding techniques for integrating classical data into quantum machine learning models, addressing a key challenge in hybrid quantum computing.

articles.title: From RTX to Spark: NVIDIA Accelerates Gemma 4 for Local Agentic AI
articles.summary: Google and NVIDIA optimized Gemma 4 models for local AI execution across devices, from RTX PCs to data centers. These small, fast models enable on-device agentic AI with real-time context processing.

articles.title: Press Start on April: GeForce NOW Brings 10 Games to the Cloud
articles.summary: GeForce NOW launches 10 new games in April, including Capcom's PRAGMATA and Arknights: Endfield. The cloud gaming service continues expanding its library with fresh titles for streaming.

articles.title: Efficiency at Scale: NVIDIA, Energy Leaders Accelerating Power‑Flexible AI Factories to Fortify the Grid
articles.summary: NVIDIA and Emerald AI unveiled flexible AI factories that function as intelligent grid assets, enabling faster grid connection, efficient operation, and improved system reliability at scale.

articles.title: Simulate realistic users to evaluate multi-turn AI agents in Strands Evals
articles.summary: Strands Evals enables systematic evaluation of multi-turn AI agents by simulating realistic user interactions. The framework assesses agent performance through evaluators measuring helpfulness, faithfulness, and tool usage.

articles.title: Scaling seismic foundation models on AWS: Distributed training with Amazon SageMaker HyperPod and expanding context windows
articles.summary: TGS partnered with AWS to optimize seismic foundation models using SageMaker HyperPod, achieving near-linear scaling for distributed training and expanding Vision Transformer context windows, reducing training time from 6 months to 5 days.

articles.title: Control which domains your AI agents can access
articles.summary: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore enables AI agents to browse the web safely by using AWS Network Firewall for domain-based filtering within VPCs, preventing unauthorized access and data exfiltration risks.

articles.title: Accelerating Vision AI Pipelines with Batch Mode VC-6 and NVIDIA Nsight
articles.summary: Vision AI pipeline performance is optimized using batch mode VC-6 and NVIDIA Nsight to accelerate decode, preprocessing, and GPU scheduling stages alongside model throughput improvements.

articles.title: Bringing AI Closer to the Edge and On-Device with Gemma 4
articles.summary: Gemma 4 launches with multimodal and multilingual capabilities, scaling from data centers to edge devices. Designed to meet growing deployment demands across NVIDIA infrastructure.

articles.title: Achieving Single-Digit Microsecond Latency Inference for Capital Markets
articles.summary: NVIDIA enables single-digit microsecond latency AI inference for algorithmic trading, helping firms compete in high-speed electronic markets using advanced neural network models instead of traditional specialized hardware.

articles.title: How generational differences affect consumer attitudes towards ads
articles.summary: A research study with CrowdDNA examines generational differences in consumer attitudes toward social media advertisements across various platforms.

articles.title: Every tree counts
articles.summary: Meta aims for net zero emissions by 2030 through developing carbon mitigation technology that will be made publicly available.

articles.title: How a non-traditional background led to cutting-edge XR tech
articles.summary: An individual with a non-traditional background pioneered innovative extended reality technology, demonstrating how unconventional paths can lead to breakthrough discoveries in emerging tech fields.

articles.title: Identifying Interactions at Scale for LLMs
articles.summary: Researchers developed methods to identify and understand interactions at scale in Large Language Models, advancing interpretability through feature attribution analysis to make AI decision-making more transparent and trustworthy.

articles.title: Information-Driven Design of Imaging Systems
articles.summary: Research presents information-driven approach to designing imaging systems that optimize how well measurements distinguish objects. Uses encoders and noise models to quantify measurement quality.

articles.title: RL without TD learning
articles.summary: A new reinforcement learning algorithm uses divide-and-conquer instead of temporal difference learning, addressing TD's scalability challenges and performing better on long-horizon tasks.
